document.title: User Manual (HnX-PB) Portable X-ray System
document.documentType: User Manual / Operation Manual and Service Information

sourceSummary.oneLine: User manual and service information for the HnX-PB battery-powered portable X-ray System manufactured by H&abyz Co., Ltd.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Provides safety precautions, indications, regulatory information, operation, maintenance, calibration, and product information for HnX-PB users and service personnel.
keyPoints: HnX-PB is a battery-powered portable X-ray System for handheld or stand-mounted diagnostic imaging with limitations of use.
The device is not intended for mammography and is not intended to replace a stationary radiographic system.
The manual lists medical electrical safety, EMC, software, usability, labeling, radio, FCC, FDA guidance, and 21 CFR ionizing radiation performance standards.
The device is specified as Class I or internally powered ME equipment, IPX0, and continuous operation.
Software/Firmware is identified as HnX_P1 version 00.12.

entities.name: HnX-PB
entities.evidence: This portable radiographic system (Model: HnX-PB) consists of an LCD display with up and down soft-keys for controlling kV, an X-ray generator (line-powered transformer), an X-ray tube assembly, a collimator and a cart.

concepts.name: Portable diagnostic X-ray equipment restricted use
concepts.pageTarget: Portable Diagnostic X-ray Equipment Restricted Use
concepts.evidence: WARNING: This Portable diagnostic X-ray equipment shall not be used for routine radiography or fluoroscopy in hospitals or private offices of health practitioners.

concepts.name: Radiation safety
concepts.pageTarget: Radiation Safety for Portable X-ray Systems
concepts.evidence: When X-ray is being irradiated, the operator shall be stand at least 1.8meters(6feet) from the patient, the X-ray tube, and out of the useful beam.

concepts.name: Electromagnetic compatibility
concepts.evidence: HnX-PB conforms to this IEC60601-1-2:2020 standard for both immunity and emissions.

concepts.name: Medical electrical equipment classification
concepts.evidence: Type of protection against electrical shock Class I or Internally powered ME equipment

concepts.name: Cybersecurity risk controls
concepts.evidence: The cybersecurity risk of the HnX-PB have been addressed to assure that no new or increased cybersecurity risks were introduced as a part of device risk analysis.

concepts.name: Unauthorized use controls
concepts.evidence: This device requires facility-implemented access controls. Failure to implement proper procedural safeguards violates radiation safety regulations

concepts.name: Preventive maintenance
concepts.evidence: The following inspection and maintenance for the unit shall be performed once every year, and handled by a service person only:

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is a portable X-ray System.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB is a portable X-ray System.

evidence.claim: The manual pertains only to the HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: This User Manual only pertains to the HnX-PB and does not serve for any other products of the company.

evidence.claim: HnX-PB is battery-powered and designed and manufactured by H&abyz.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is a battery-powered portable X-ray System, designed and manufactured by H&abyz.

evidence.claim: The device is for X-ray imaging and diagnosis in facilities with portable or fixing sites.
evidence.evidenceText: The system is for X-ray imaging and diagnosis in facilities with portable or fixing sites.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is not intended for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: This HnX-PB is not intended for mammography.

evidence.claim: Indications include handheld diagnostic imaging of body extremities and stand-mounted imaging of head, abdomen, extremities, and chest without a grid.
evidence.evidenceText: The device may be used for handheld diagnostic imaging of body extremities. The device may be used for stand mounted diagnostic imaging of head, abdomen, or extremities. The device may be used for stand mounted imaging of the chest when used without a grid.

evidence.claim: The device is not for bariatric patients unless imaging body extremities.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used on bariatric patients, unless imaging body extremities

evidence.claim: The device is not intended to replace a stationary radiographic system.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not intended to replace a stationary radiographic system, which may be required for full optimization of image quality and radiation exposure for different exam types.

evidence.claim: Portable diagnostic X-ray equipment shall not be used for routine radiography or fluoroscopy in hospitals or private offices of health practitioners.
evidence.evidenceText: WARNING: This Portable diagnostic X-ray equipment shall not be used for routine radiography or fluoroscopy in hospitals or private offices of health practitioners.
evidence.pageTarget: Portable Diagnostic X-ray Equipment Restricted Use

evidence.claim: The device emits ionizing radiation and lacks built-in user authentication.
evidence.evidenceText: This device emits ionizing radiation and lacks built-in user authentication. Unauthorized operation may result in inappropriate radiation exposure to patients, operators, or bystanders

evidence.claim: Facilities must implement access controls and maintain a current written list of authorized operators.
evidence.evidenceText: Facilities must maintain a current written list of all authorized operators including: Full name, license number and expiration date, training completion date etc.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is specified as Class I and IPX0 equipment under EN 60601-1 standards.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB is specified as Class I and IPX0 equipment under EN 60601-1 standards.

evidence.claim: The regulatory section lists IEC 60601-1, IEC 60601-1-2, IEC 60601-1-3, IEC 60601-2-28, IEC 60601-2-54, IEC 62304, IEC 62366-1, IEC 60601-1-6, ISO 15223-1, radio EMC, FCC, and 21 CFR standards or regulations.
evidence.evidenceText: Product safety standards IEC 60601-1: 2005/AMD2:2020 ... IEC 60601-1-2:2020 / EN60601-1-2:2021 ... IEC 62304:2006/AMD1:2015 ... FCC SUB PART C ... 21 CFR 1020 Subchapter J ... 21 CFR 1020.30

evidence.claim: HnX-PB conforms to IEC 60601-1-2:2020 for immunity and emissions.
evidence.evidenceText: HnX-PB conforms to this IEC60601-1-2:2020 standard for both immunity and emissions.

evidence.claim: Software or firmware updates must be performed by authorized service personnel or by returning the product to the manufacturer or service center.
evidence.evidenceText: the product's software and firmware updates cannot be performed directly by users in the field, and must be performed by authorized service personnel or by returning the product to the manufacturer or service center.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB software/firmware is HnX_P1 version 00.12.
evidence.evidenceText: Software(Firmware): HnX_P1 Version: 00.12

evidence.claim: The maximum output of the X-ray generator is 720 W.
evidence.evidenceText: The maximum output of X-ray generator is 720W

evidence.claim: The X-ray tube output voltage range is 50 to 90 kV.
evidence.evidenceText: The X-ray Tube output voltage of generator is 50~90kV

evidence.claim: The X-ray tube focal spot is 0.5 mm x 0.5 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: The X-ray Tube focal spot is 0.5㎜ x 0.5㎜

evidence.claim: The product lifetime is estimated up to 7 years under appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.
evidence.evidenceText: The estimated product lifetime is up to 7 years under appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.

evidence.claim: Regular inspection should be performed at least once a year.
evidence.evidenceText: be sure to perform regular inspection at least once a year.

evidence.claim: Annual preventive inspection and maintenance must be handled by a service person only.
evidence.evidenceText: The following inspection and maintenance for the unit shall be performed once every year, and handled by a service person only:

evidence.claim: The product is identified as a medical device and made in Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturing country: Made in Korea This product is a medical device

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not present in the provided source text or metadata.
Revision/version is inferred from the sourcePath filename '(Rev 0.1)' rather than a separate document body field.

Authority signal is 'general' and the document lists multiple jurisdictions and authorities, including FCC, FDA-related guidance, 21 CFR, EU-related recycling language, IEC, EN, ETSI, and KS standards.

Indication and contraindication text may conflict: indications allow stand-mounted imaging of head, abdomen, extremities, and chest without a grid, while contraindication states it should only be used for imaging body extremities and cannot be used for any other purpose.
Radiation distance statements vary by section: 1.8 meters from patient/X-ray tube, at least 2 meters between operator and unit, and a minimum safe distance of 20 cm from the target.
Source text contains OCR or extraction artifacts and typos such as duplicated pediatric caution, 'KULL', 'Warring ex time', and repeated or inconsistent section numbering.
